title: Anumolu Kartheek vs M/S. Sunseed Hospitals on 21 November, 2025
body: Anumolu Kartheek vs M/S. Sunseed Hospitals on 21 November, 2025 Author: K. Lakshman Bench: K. Lakshman THE HONOURABLE SRI JUSTICE K. LAKSHMAN CIVIL REVISION PETITION No.4171 of 2025 ORDER: Heard Sri Mandapati Murali Krishna, learned counsel for the petitioner. 2. This Civil Revision Petition is filed under Article 227 of the Constitution of India, seeking a direction to club O.S. No. 542 of 2024, on the file of the learned III Additional Junior Civil Judge, Malkajgiri, Medchal-Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda, with O.S. No. 22 of 2025, on the file of the learned Principal District and Sessions Judge-cum-Family Court, Medchal- Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda and to dispose of the same in accordance with law as expeditiously as possible by fixing time- bound. 3. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner has filed a suit in O.S. No. 22 of 2025 on the file of the Principal District and Sessions Judge-cum-Family Court, Medchal-Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda, against the respondent seeking an order of eviction and recovery of rents. In the said suit, he has filed an interlocutory application vide I.A. 2 No. 1 of 2025, under Section 15-A of the C.P.C., seeking a direction to the respondent to deposit the admitted rents. However, the trial Court is not disposing of the said interlocutory application in accordance with law. It is further submitted that the respondent has already filed a suit vide O.S. No. 542 of 2024, on the file of the III Additional Junior Civil Judge, Malkajgiri, Medchal-Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda, against the petitioner seeking an injunction. Therefore, the petitioner is seeking a direction to the Principal District and Sessions Judge- cum-Family Court, Medchal-Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda, to club both the suits and decide the same, along with the interlocutory applications pending therein, as expeditiously as possible. 4. As discussed supra, the suit filed by the petitioner in O.S. No. 22 of 2025 against the respondent, seeking eviction and payment of rents, is pending on the file of the Principal District and Sessions Judge-cum-Family Court, Medchal-Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda. The suit filed by the respondent in O.S. No. 542 of 2024 against the petitioner, seeking an injunction, is pending on the file of the III Additional Junior Civil Judge, 3 Malkajgiri, Medchal-Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda. There is no provision in the C.P.C . for clubbing both suits; at the most, the petitioner can seek consolidation of the said suits. However, both suits are pending before different Courts. Be that as it may, the Principal District and Sessions Judge-cum-Family Court, Medchal-Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda, has to dispose of I.A. No. 1 of 2025 filed by the petitioner under Section 15-A of the C.P.C. in accordance with law. The petitioner filed the said I.A. in January 2025. 5. During the course of hearing, it is brought to the notice of this Court that the respondent has already filed counter in I.A.No.1 of 2025 in O.S.No.22 of 2025. 6. In the light of the aforesaid discussion, this Civil Revision Petition is disposed of at the admission stage, waiving notice to the respondents, by invoking the powers of this Court under Article 227 of the Constitution of India. The Principal District and Sessions Judge-cum-Family Court, Medchal-Malkajgiri District at Kushaiguda, shall make an endeavour to dispose of I.A. No. 1 of 2025 in O.S. No. 22 of 2025, filed by the petitioner under Section 15-A of the C.P.C., in accordance with law, as 4 expeditiously as possible, preferably within a period of one month from the date of receipt of a copy of this order. Liberty is granted to the petitioner to seek consolidation of both suits by filing an appropriate application, and it is for the Court to decide the said application in accordance with law. There shall be no order as to costs. As a sequel, miscellaneous petitions pending, if any, shall stand closed. _____________________ K. LAKSHMAN, J 21.11.2025 sa
